name: babelfont

metadata.name: babelfont
metadata.summary: Load, examine and save fonts in a variety of formats

documentation:
# Babelfont: Load, examine and save fonts in a variety of formats

*This describes Babelfont >3.0, which is a complete rewrite from the previous version.*

Babelfont is a utility for loading fonts and examining fonts in a variety
of formats. It can also be used to *write* fonts in some of these formats,
making it possible to convert between font formats.

Here are the formats which are currently supported:

| Format          | Read    | Write |
|-----------------|---------|-------|
| Glyphs 2        | partial |       |
| Glyphs 3        | *       | *     |
| .glyphspackage  | *       |       |
| UFO             | *       |       |
| Designspace     | *       |       |
| Fontlab VFJ     | partial |       |
| Fontlab VFB     | partial |       |
| TTF             | partial | *     |
| OTF             | partial |       |
| Fontforge SFD   | partial | *     |
| Fontforge SFDir | partial | *     |
| Babelfont       | *       | *     |

Babelfont converts all of the above font formats into a intermediary
set of objects, whose object hierarchy can be seen [here](https://simoncozens.github.io/babelfont). The allows
the developer to examine any font (single master or variable), without
needing to worry about the details of each font format.

For example:

```python
from babelfont import load

font = load("Myfont.glyphs") # Or .designspace, or whatever
default_a = font.default_master.get_glyph_layer("A")
top_anchor = default_a.anchors_dict["top"].x
print("Top anchor = (%i,%i)" % (top_anchor.x, top_anchor.y))
print("LSB, RSB = (%i,%i)" % (default_a.lsb, default_a.rsb))
font.save("Myfont.ttf")
```

classes.name: babelfont.convertors.nfsf.Babelfont
documentation: One would hope this would be easy.

classes.name: babelfont.Features.Features
documentation: A representation of the OpenType feature code.

classes.name: babelfont.Axis.Axis

methods.name: babelfont.Axis.Axis.normalize_value
documentation:
Return a normalized co-ordinate (-1.0 to 1.0) for the given value.
The value provided is expected to be in userspace coordinates.

methods.name: babelfont.Axis.Axis.denormalize_value
documentation: Return a userspace coordinate for the given normalized value.

methods.name: babelfont.Axis.Axis.maximum
documentation: Compatibility with designspaceLib. Our names are smaller for serialization.

methods.name: babelfont.Axis.Axis.inverted_map
documentation:
Return the axis map as a list of tuples, where the first value is the
designspace coordinate and the second value is the userspace coordinate.

methods.name: babelfont.Axis.Axis.map_forward
documentation: Map a location on this axis from userspace to designspace.

methods.name: babelfont.Axis.Axis.map_backward
documentation: Map a location on this axis from designspace to userspace.

methods.name: babelfont.Axis.Axis.userspace_to_designspace
documentation: Map a location on this axis from userspace to designspace.

methods.name: babelfont.Axis.Axis.designspace_to_userspace
documentation: Map a location on this axis from designspace to userspace.
Represents an axis in a multiple master or variable font.

modules.name: babelfont.fontFilters.featureWriters
documentation: from fontFeatures import Attachment, Routine, Positioning, ValueRecord

functions.name: babelfont.fontFilters.anchorPropagation._get_anchor_data
documentation: Get data for an anchor from a list of components.

functions.name: babelfont.fontFilters.anchorPropagation._adjust_anchors
documentation:
Adjust base anchors to which a mark component may have been attached, by
moving the base anchor attached to a mark anchor to the position of
the mark component's base anchor.

functions.name: babelfont.fontFilters.anchorPropagation._component_closest_to_origin
documentation:
Return the component whose (xmin, ymin) bounds are closest to origin.

This ensures that a component that is moved below another is
actually recognized as such. Looking only at the transformation
offset can be misleading.

functions.name: babelfont.fontFilters.fillOpentype.fill_opentype_values
documentation:
Prepare a font for final compilation by moving values from
font attributes to the customOpenTypeValues field.

functions.name: babelfont.fontFilters.intermediateLayer.intermediate_location
documentation: We know a layer is "intermediate" if it has a Glyphs-specific attribute
["attr"]["coordinates"]. The coordinates are the location of the sparse master;
just the raw values, so we zip them with the axis tags in order.

classes.name: babelfont.Names.Names

class_variables.name: babelfont.Names.Names.familyName
documentation: Name ID 1 if styleMapFamilyName is not present
A table of global, localizable names for the font.

classes.name: babelfont.Instance.Instance
documentation: An object representing a named or static instance.

classes.name: babelfont.Font.Font

methods.name: babelfont.Font.Font.save
documentation:
Save the font to a file. The file type is determined by the extension.
Any additional keyword arguments are passed to the save method of the
appropriate converter.

methods.name: babelfont.Font.Font.master
documentation: Locates a master by its ID. Returns `None` if not found.

methods.name: babelfont.Font.Font.map_forward
documentation: Map a location (dictionary of `tag: number`) from userspace to designspace.

methods.name: babelfont.Font.Font.map_backward
documentation: Map a location (dictionary of `tag: number`) from designspace to userspace.

methods.name: babelfont.Font.Font.userspace_to_designspace
documentation: Map a location (dictionary of `tag: number`) from userspace to designspace.

methods.name: babelfont.Font.Font.designspace_to_userspace
documentation: Map a location (dictionary of `tag: number`) from designspace to userspace.

methods.name: babelfont.Font.Font.default_master
documentation:
Return the default master. If there is only one master, return it.
If there are multiple masters, return the one with the default location.
If there is no default location, raise an error.

methods.name: babelfont.Font.Font.unicode_map
documentation: Return a dictionary mapping Unicode codepoints to glyph names.

methods.name: babelfont.Font.Font.variation_model
documentation:
Return a `fontTools.varLib.models.VariationModel` object representing
the font's axes and masters. This is used for generating variable fonts.

methods.name: babelfont.Font.Font.get_variable_anchor
documentation:
Return a tuple of `VariableScalar` objects representing the x and y
coordinates of the anchor on the given glyph. The `VariableScalar` objects
are indexed by master location. If the anchor is not found on some master,
raise an `IncompatibleMastersError`.

methods.name: babelfont.Font.Font.exported_glyphs
documentation: Return a list of glyph names that are marked for export.
Represents a font, with one or more masters.
